text: The surgeon retracts the bladder upwards towards the twelve o'clock position, revealing the space between the bladder and the vagina.
The surgeon uses a prograsper to manipulate the vagina, while two needle drivers are visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon uses a prograsper to release tension proximally, adjusting the tissue to achieve the desired tension for placation.
The surgeon performs seven to twelve placations, moving from left to right and distal to proximal, adjusting the technique based on the patient's anatomy.
The surgeon passes a suture through the distal aspect of the right uterosacral ligament, joining it to the vaginal tissue.
The surgeon places the mesh on the anterior and posterior aspects of the surgical site, ensuring proper coverage for hernia repair.
